name: rate_limited_agent
description: an agent respecting API rate limits world; actions request, backoff, retry

card.description: an agent respecting API rate limits world; actions request, backoff, retry

rules: The API is a token bucket of size `capacity`; every `refill_interval` steps it refills `refill_per_tick` tokens up to `capacity`, a sustained rate strictly below one request per step.
A `request` with tokens available spends one token and completes successfully, resetting `backoff` to 1.
A `request` with an empty bucket is rate limited (429): it increments `throttled`, queues the call in `pending`, and obliges a `backoff`.
A respectful agent never issues a `request` while `cooldown > 0`; doing so is `blocked` and accomplishes nothing.
`backoff` honors Retry-After: it arms `cooldown` for `backoff` ticks and then doubles `backoff`, capped at `max_backoff`.
`retry` re-drives one queued request once `cooldown` has elapsed and a token is free; if still limited it re-arms an exponential backoff.

transition.code:
def transition(state, action):
    s = dict(state)
    name = action["name"] if isinstance(action, dict) else action
    cap = s["capacity"]
    # every step advances time; the bucket refills `refill_per_tick` tokens once
    # every `refill_interval` steps (a sustained-rate limiter that is strictly
    # slower than 1 request/step, so bursts genuinely drain it). Any outstanding
    # cooldown (a respected Retry-After window) ticks down by one each step.
    s["time"] = s["time"] + 1
    if s["time"] % s["refill_interval"] == 0:
        s["tokens"] = min(cap, s["tokens"] + s["refill_per_tick"])
    if s["cooldown"] > 0:
        s["cooldown"] = s["cooldown"] - 1

    if name == "request":
        if s["cooldown"] > 0:
            # agent is inside a backoff window: a polite agent does not call.
            s["last_status"] = "blocked"
        elif s["tokens"] >= 1:
            s["tokens"] = s["tokens"] - 1
            s["completed"] = s["completed"] + 1
            s["backoff"] = 1            # success resets exponential backoff
            s["last_status"] = "ok"
        else:
            # bucket empty -> 429; queue the call and require a backoff next.
            s["throttled"] = s["throttled"] + 1
            s["pending"] = s["pending"] + 1
            s["last_status"] = "429"

    elif name == "backoff":
        # honor Retry-After: wait `backoff` ticks, then double it (capped).
        s["cooldown"] = min(s["max_backoff"], s["backoff"])
        s["backoff"] = min(s["max_backoff"], s["backoff"] * 2)
        s["last_status"] = "wait"

    elif name == "retry":
        if s["pending"] <= 0:
            s["last_status"] = "idle"          # nothing queued to retry
        elif s["cooldown"] > 0:
            s["last_status"] = "wait"          # still inside backoff window
        elif s["tokens"] >= 1:
            s["tokens"] = s["tokens"] - 1
            s["pending"] = s["pending"] - 1
            s["completed"] = s["completed"] + 1
            s["backoff"] = 1                   # drained one from the queue
            s["last_status"] = "ok"
        else:
            # still rate limited: re-arm an (exponentially longer) backoff.
            s["throttled"] = s["throttled"] + 1
            s["cooldown"] = min(s["max_backoff"], s["backoff"])
            s["backoff"] = min(s["max_backoff"], s["backoff"] * 2)
            s["last_status"] = "429"

    return s

emit.report: t={time}: completed={completed}, 429s={throttled}, pending={pending}, tokens={tokens}, cooldown={cooldown}, status={last_status}
